steps.title: Identify the punctuation rule being tested
steps.content: The sentence lists three works, each followed by a descriptive phrase containing its own comma: The Joys of Motherhood, a novel...; A Kind of Marriage, a television play...; and Head Above Water, her autobiography. Because the items contain internal commas, the rule for a complex series applies: use semicolons (not commas) to separate the items from each other, and use a comma after each title to introduce its description.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice by the rule violated
steps.content:
The blank fills the slot between "Lagos" (end of item 1's description) and A Kind of Marriage (start of item 2). A semicolon must go after "Lagos" to separate items 1 and 2, and a comma must follow A Kind of Marriage to introduce its description.
  • Choice A — Lagos, A Kind of Marriage,: uses a comma after "Lagos" to separate items in a series that already has internal commas — violates the complex-series rule requiring semicolons between items. ✗
  • Choice B — Lagos; A Kind of Marriage,: semicolon after "Lagos" correctly separates items 1 and 2; comma after the title correctly introduces the description. Matches the complex-series rule. ✓
  • Choice C — Lagos, A Kind of Marriage:: comma after "Lagos" again fails the complex-series rule, and a colon cannot separate a title from its supplementary description mid-series. ✗
  • Choice D — Lagos; A Kind of Marriage: semicolon after "Lagos" is correct, but omitting the comma after the title leaves no punctuation between the title and its description, violating the requirement to set off the supplementary phrase. ✗
That is the correct answer.
